title: Ron Mueck
summary: Ron Mueck's sculptures manipulate scale to evoke emotional ambiguity and reflection.
description: Ron Mueck's exhibition at the Mori Art Museum showcases his mastery in manipulating scale, presenting sculptures that are both hyper-realistic and surreal. His works, crafted with meticulous attention to detail, capture the essence of human emotions while maintaining an enigmatic quality that invites personal interpretation.

Visitors will find themselves drawn into a contemplative experience, as Mueck's sculptures challenge perceptions of reality and evoke a range of emotions. The interplay of scale and detail encourages viewers to engage deeply with the pieces, fostering a unique and introspective journey through the human condition.
highlights: Innovative manipulation of scale in figurative sculptures
Second solo exhibition in Japan since 2008
Previously shown in Paris, Milan, and Seoul
access: Roppongi Station (Hibiya Line / Oedo Line), direct access to Roppongi Hills

title: Roppongi Crossing 2025: What Passes Is Time. We Are Eternal.
summary: Roppongi Crossing 2025 features a unique blend of Asian and Japanese-rooted artists exploring 'time'.
description: This eighth edition of Roppongi Crossing stands out with its collaborative curatorial approach, featuring both Mori Art Museum curators and two internationally active Asian guest curators. The exhibition showcases a diverse array of twenty-one artists and artist groups, offering a multifaceted exploration of the theme 'time' through various mediums including painting, sculpture, video, crafts, and community projects.

Visitors will experience a dynamic and layered presentation that transcends traditional art boundaries, inviting them to engage with the concept of time from multiple cultural and artistic perspectives. The inclusion of artists with Japanese roots based overseas adds a global dimension, enriching the dialogue between local and international contemporary art practices.
highlights: Collaborative curatorial team with Asian guest curators
Includes crafts and community projects alongside traditional mediums
Features artists with Japanese roots based overseas
access: Roppongi Station (Hibiya Line / Oedo Line), direct access to Roppongi Hills

title: MAM Collection 021: Hao Jingban
summary: Hao Jingban's exhibition uniquely delves into history through archival research and personal narratives.
description: Hao Jingban's exhibition at the Mori Art Museum is visually distinctive for its integration of video works that blend archival research with personal interviews and performances. This approach creates a layered narrative that uncovers the complexities of historical events and their impact on individual lives.

Visitors are invited into a reflective space where the past and present converge, offering a nuanced perspective on how history informs contemporary experiences. The exhibition fosters a contemplative atmosphere, encouraging viewers to consider the intricate connections between personal and collective memories.
highlights: Video works combining archival research with personal interviews
Focus on individual and collective historical narratives
Integration of performance elements in video works
access: Roppongi Station (Hibiya Line / Oedo Line), direct access to Roppongi Hills
